questions.question: Four number sets have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the number set that is different from the rest. [NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.]
In a certain code language, 'APPLE' is written as '2813131724', 'APPRAISAL' is written as '281313112820102817', how will 'IMPACT' be written in that language?
What would be the symbol on the opposite side of ‘&’ if the given sheet is folded to form a cube? ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997040a037e13.25181147.png)

questions.question: Study the given diagram carefully and answer the question that follows. The numbers in different sections indicate the number of families who visited different places [Srinagar, Shimla, Gangtok]. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997040aa89b69.41898093.png) What is the total number of families who visited at least two of the places out of Srinagar, Shimla, Gangtok ?
questions.explanation: Number of families who visited any of two of the places = 7+5+22 = 34 Number of families who visited all three places = 15 Therefore, number of families who visited at least two of the places = 34+15 = 49

questions.question: If ‘+’ is interchanged with ‘×’, and ‘–’ is interchanged with ‘÷’, then which of the following equations is correct ?
questions.explanation: After interchanging the sign in option B 18 × 6 + 24 ÷ 4 – 64 = 50 108 + 6 – 64 = 50 114 – 64 = 50 50 = 50 LHS = RHS

E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /deleting /multiplying etc., to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.]

Words given on the left side of [::] are related with each other by some Logic/Rule/Relation. Select the missing word/word pair on the right side of [::] from the given alternatives based on the same Logic/Rule/Relation. Pen : Author :: Axe : ?
questions.explanation: Pen is used by Author in his profession. Similarly, Axe is used by Woodcutter.

questions.question: The position of how many letters will change if each of the letters in the word HYPERTENSION is arranged from left to right in alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: HYPERTENSION EEHINNOPRSTY [The positions of all 12 letters have been changed.]

questions.question: Seven people A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre [but not necessarily in the same order]. C is sitting second to the left of A. Only one person is sitting between B and C when counted from right of B. E is sitting third to the right of F. D is sitting to the immediate left of E. The position of how many persons excluding A will remain unchanged if all the persons are arranged in the English alphabetical order in clockwise direction, starting from A, keeping the position of A unchanged?
questions.explanation: Sol: As per the directions of the question, the order is: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997040ceac3e7.79980846.png) After rearranging in the English alphabetical order in clockwise direction starting from A; ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997040d09a923.54264286.png) It can clearly be seen that the position of only C is intact other than A. Hence, option D.

[NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.]

questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Order as per Dictionary: Boater, Boathouse, Boating, Boatman, Boatyard

questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997040dc5dd63.09189240.png) Hence, Uncle’s son Ritik will be Cousin of Sunaina.

questions.explanation: First Term: +2, +1, +2, +1, +2 Second Term: –2, –1, –2, –1, –2 J+2 = L, Q–2 = O ;; LO [option B]

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters that when placed from left to right in the blanks below will complete the given letter series. L M N _ O L L M _ Q O L L _ N Q O _ L M N Q _ L L M
questions.explanation: L M N Q O L / L M N Q O L / L M N Q O L / L M N Q O L / L M i.e. QNMLO

Which letter cluster does not belong to that group? [Note: The odd letter-cluster is not based on the number of consonants / vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.]

Select the option that is related to the fourth number in the same way as the first number is related to the second number and the fifth number is related to the sixth number. 22 : 132 :: ? : 72 :: 24 : 144

Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follows/follow from the statements. Statements: I. All buildings are towers. II. No tower is a shop. III. Some shops are offices. Conclusions: I. Some offices are towers. II. No building is a shop.

questions.question: If 20 February 2012 was Sunday, then what was the day of the week on 20 February 2031?
questions.explanation: Date & Month is same so Operation applied only on Year: Calculation of total odd days: 31-12 = 19 years = 19 odd days 2012, 2016, 2020, 2024, 2028 5 leap year = 5 extra odd days Total odd days = 24 or say 3 odd days So Correct answer will be Sunday+3 = Wednesday

questions.question: What is the name of the active volcano in India?
questions.explanation: The name of the active volcano in India is Barren Island. Barren Island, located in the Andaman Sea about 135 km North-East of Port Blair in the Andaman Islands. The volcanoes sit on the Burma [or Burmese] Plate.

questions.question: Which of the following conditions is NOT caused by viruses?
questions.explanation: Cholera is an acute diarrheal illness caused by infection of the intestine with Vibrio cholera bacteria. HIV [human immunodeficiency virus] is a virus that attacks the body's immune system. If HIV is not treated, it can lead to AIDS [acquired immunodeficiency syndrome]. Dengue viruses are spread to people through the bite of an infected Aedes species mosquito. The virus responsible for causing dengue is called dengue virus [DENV].

questions.question: In which year was the Indian national anthem sung for the first time at the Indian National Congress Session?
questions.explanation: In 1911, The Indian national anthem was sung for the first time at the Indian National Congress Session. National anthem was originally written in Bengali by Rabindranath Tagore and adopted in Hindi by the constitution assembly in 1950.

questions.question: Which colour is associated with the revolution in fertilizers?
questions.explanation: Grey colour is associated with the revolution in fertilisers that is increased fertilizer production which is basically related to the maleffect of the green revolution. "

questions.question: Pattachitra style of painting is one of the oldest and most popular art forms of which of the following states?
questions.explanation: Pattachitra style of painting is one of the oldest and most popular art forms of Odisha. The pattachitra art form is known for its intricate details as well as mythological narratives and folktales inscribed in it.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of pressure?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of pressure is Pascal. Radian is the unit of Angle. Steradian is the unit of Solid Angle. Ampere is the unit of Current. > दबाव की SI इकाई पास्कल है। रेडियन कोण की इकाई है। स्टेरेडियन ठोस कोण की इकाई है। एम्पीयर धारा की इकाई है।

questions.question: Which statement from the given options is NOT correct?
options: Upagupta influenced Ashoka to embrace Buddhism.>उपगुप्त ने अशोक को बौद्ध धर्म अपनाने के लिए प्रभावित किया
Buddhist scriptures were written in Pali.>बौद्ध ग्रंथ पाली में लिखे गए थे।
Charak was the personal physician of Gautam Buddha.>चरक गौतम बुद्ध के निजी चिकित्सक थे।
The birthplace of Gautam Buddha is in Nepal.>गौतम बुद्ध का जन्मस्थान नेपाल में है।
questions.explanation: Jivaka was the personal physician of Gautam Buddha. Charaka was one of the principal contributors to Ayurveda, a system of medicine and lifestyle developed in Ancient India. > जीवक गौतम बुद्ध की निजी चिकित्सक थीं। चरक आयुर्वेद में प्रमुख योगदानकर्ताओं में से एक था, जो प्राचीन भारत में विकसित चिकित्सा और जीवन शैली की एक प्रणाली थी

questions.question: Which organization was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2024 for its efforts to achieve a world free of nuclear weapons?
options: International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons [ICAN]
questions.explanation: In 2024, the Nobel Peace Prize was awarded to Nihon Hidankyo, the Japanese organization of atomic bomb survivors from Hiroshima and Nagasaki, also known as Hibakusha. The Norwegian Nobel Committee recognized their efforts to achieve a world free of nuclear weapons and for demonstrating through witness testimony that nuclear weapons must never be used again. > 2024 में, हिरोशिमा और नागासाकी के परमाणु बम बचे लोगों के जापानी संगठन निहोन हिदांक्यो को नोबेल शांति पुरस्कार से सम्मानित किया गया, जिसे हिबाकुशा के नाम से भी जाना जाता है। नॉर्वेजियन नोबेल समिति ने परमाणु हथियारों से मुक्त दुनिया को प्राप्त करने और गवाहों की गवाही के माध्यम से प्रदर्शित करने के उनके प्रयासों को मान्यता दी कि परमाणु हथियारों का फिर कभी इस्तेमाल नहीं किया जाना चाहिए।

questions.question: Which of the following is called the father of supercomputing?
questions.explanation: Computers with fast processing power and large storage capacity [memory] are called supercomputers. It is used for research and design in scientific and technical fields, for locating oil deposits in the petroleum industry and for forecasting the weather in meteorology. Seymour Royer Cray [American] is considered the father of supercomputing. He founded the Cray research company by which the world's first supercomputer Cray K-LS was built in 1979. > तेज प्रसंस्करण शक्ति और बड़ी भंडारण क्षमता [मेमोरी[ वाले कंप्यूटर सुपर कंप्यूटर कहलाते हैं। इसका उपयोग वैज्ञानिक और तकनीकी क्षेत्रों में अनुसंधान और डिजाइन के लिए, पेट्रोलियम उद्योग में तेल भंडार का पता लगाने और मौसम विज्ञान में मौसम की भविष्यवाणी के लिए किया जाता है। सीमोर रॉयर क्रे [अमेरिकी] को सुपरकंप्यूटिंग का जनक माना जाता है। उन्होंने क्रे रिसर्च कंपनी की स्थापना की जिसके द्वारा 1979 में दुनिया का पहला सुपर कंप्यूटर क्रे के-एलएस बनाया गया था।

questions.question: The Aravalli Range does not pass through _____ state of India?
questions.explanation: Spread over an area of 692 km, the Aravallis cover the states of Gujarat, Rajasthan, Delhi and Haryana. Satpura Ranges covers Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh and Gujarat. > 692 किलोमीटर के क्षेत्र में फैली अरावली गुजरात, राजस्थान, दिल्ली और हरियाणा राज्यों को कवर करती है। सतपुड़ा पर्वतमाला में मध्य प्रदेश, महाराष्ट्र, छत्तीसगढ़ और गुजरात शामिल हैं।

questions.question: Who laid the foundation stone of the world - famous Swarna Mandir [Golden Temple]?
options: Hazrat Mian Mir Ji>हजरत मियां मीर जी
questions.explanation: Hazrat Mian Mir Ji laid the foundation stone of the world – famous Swarna Mandir (Golden Temple). The Golden Temple, known as the Harmandir in India, was built in 1604 by Guru Arjun. Amritsar was founded by Sri Guru Ram Das ji, the fourth guru of the Sikhs in about 1574 A.D. > हजरत मियां मीर जी ने विश्व प्रसिद्ध स्वर्ण मंदिर स्वर्ण मंदिर की आधारशिला रखी। स्वर्ण मंदिर, जिसे भारत में हरमंदिर के रूप में जाना जाता है, 1604 में गुरु अर्जुन द्वारा बनाया गया था। अमृतसर की स्थापना सिखों के चौथे गुरु श्री गुरु राम दास जी ने लगभग 1574 ईस्वी में की थी।

questions.question: Which of the following is a basic structural and functional unit of the nervous system?
questions.explanation: Neurons are a basic structural and functional unit of the nervous system. Nephron is the functional unit of the kidney. The nasopalatine nerve is a nerve of the head. Nephric is related to or connected with a kidney. > न्यूरॉन्स तंत्रिका तंत्र की एक बुनियादी संरचनात्मक और कार्यात्मक इकाई हैं। नेफ्रॉन गुर्दे की कार्यात्मक इकाई है। नासोपलाटिन तंत्रिका सिर की एक तंत्रिका है। नेफ्रिक एक गुर्दे से संबंधित या जुड़ा हुआ है।

questions.question: The Law of Octaves’ was proposed by:?
questions.explanation: The Law of Octaves’ was proposed by Alexander Newlands in 1865. If the chemical elements are arranged according to increasing atomic weight, those with similar physical and chemical properties occur after each interval of seven elements. An example of the Law of Octaves is lithium, sodium, and potassium; they share physical and chemical properties. > 'अष्टक का नियम' 1865 में अलेक्जेंडर न्यूलैंड्स द्वारा प्रस्तावित किया गया था। यदि रासायनिक तत्वों को बढ़ते परमाणु भार के अनुसार व्यवस्थित किया जाता है, तो समान भौतिक और रासायनिक गुणों वाले सात तत्वों के प्रत्येक अंतराल के बाद होते हैं। अष्टक के नियम का एक उदाहरण लिथियम, सोडियम और पोटेशियम है; वे भौतिक और रासायनिक गुणों को साझा करते हैं।

questions.question: Which commission suggested that jobs in the Central Government should be reserved for the 'Other Backward Classes' [OBC]?
questions.explanation: The Mandal Commission was established in India on 1st January 1979 by Janata Party and submitted its report in 1980. > मंडल आयोग की स्थापना भारत में 1 जनवरी 1979 को जनता पार्टी द्वारा की गई थी और 1980 में अपनी रिपोर्ट प्रस्तुत की थी।

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the SMARTH scheme launched by the Government of India?
options: To promote smart cities development in India >भारत में स्मार्ट शहरों के विकास को बढ़ावा देना
To support the modernization and growth of the handicrafts and handloom sector >हस्तशिल्प और हथकरघा क्षेत्र के आधुनिकीकरण और विकास का समर्थन करना
To enhance digital literacy among rural populations>ग्रामीण आबादी के बीच डिजिटल साक्षरता बढ़ाने के लिए
To provide financial assistance for MSME startups>MSME स्टार्टअप के लिए वित्तीय सहायता प्रदान करना
questions.explanation: The SMARTH [Scheme for Modernization and Rejuvenation of Textiles and Handicrafts] scheme was launched in 2024 by the Ministry of Textiles, Government of India. It aims to support artisans and weavers by providing financial assistance, infrastructure development, and skill enhancement to promote the traditional handicrafts and handloom industry in India. > SMARTH [वस्त्र और हस्तशिल्प के आधुनिकीकरण और कायाकल्प के लिए योजना] योजना 2024 में कपड़ा मंत्रालय, भारत सरकार द्वारा शुरू की गई थी। इसका उद्देश्य भारत में पारंपरिक हस्तशिल्प और हथकरघा उद्योग को बढ़ावा देने के लिए वित्तीय सहायता, बुनियादी ढांचे के विकास और कौशल वृद्धि प्रदान करके कारीगरों और बुनकरों का समर्थन करना है।

questions.question: Match the following rivers with their respective origin places: Rivers Origin Places (A) Narmada • Talakaveri (B) Godavari • Trimbakeshwar (C) Krishna • Mahabaleshwar (D) Cauvery • Amarkantak
questions.explanation: Narmada River – Originates from the Amarkantak Plateau, Madhya Pradesh. It is one of the major west-flowing rivers of India and is considered sacred. Godavari River – Originates from Trimbakeshwar, Maharashtra. It is the longest river in peninsular India and is often referred to as the Dakshina Ganga [Ganga of the South]. Krishna River – Originates from Mahabaleshwar, Maharashtra. It is one of the longest rivers in India, flowing through Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh. Cauvery [Kaveri] River – Originates from Talakaveri, Karnataka. It is an important river in southern India and is known for its role in irrigation and hydroelectric power generation.

questions.question: Which model was the Second Five Year Plan based on?
questions.explanation: Second five year plan is based on PC Mahalanobis Model. Indian statistician Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis is the architect of the Second Five-Year Plan [1953]. It is focused on the development of the public sector and rapid industrialisation.

questions.question: Who among the following wrote the book ‘Hind Swaraj’?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi wrote the book ‘Hind Swaraj’ or Indian Home Rule in 1909. The book was banned by the British Government in 1910 in India as a seditious text. > महात्मा गांधी ने 1909 में 'हिंद स्वराज' या भारतीय गृह नियम पुस्तक लिखी थी। इस पुस्तक को ब्रिटिश सरकार ने 1910 में भारत में राजद्रोह के रूप में प्रतिबंधित कर दिया था।

questions.question: Who was the runner-up in the Pro Kabaddi League Season 11 held in 2024?
questions.explanation: In the 2024 Pro Kabaddi League Season 11, the Haryana Steelers clinched their maiden title by defeating the Patna Pirates with a score of 32-23 in the final. > 2024 प्रो कबड्डी लीग सीजन 11 में, हरियाणा स्टीलर्स ने फाइनल में पटना पाइरेट्स को 32-23 के स्कोर से हराकर अपना पहला खिताब जीता।

questions.question: Which Maharaja of Manipur signed the Instrument of Accession with the Government of India?
questions.explanation: The Maharaja of Manipur, Bodhachandra Singh signed the 'Instrument of Accession' with the Indian government on the assurance that internal autonomy of Manipur would be maintained. The Maharaja held elections in Manipur in June 1948 and the state became a constitutional monarchy. Thus Manipur was the first part of India to hold an election based on Universal Adult Franchise. > मणिपुर के महाराजा बोधचंद्र सिंह ने भारत सरकार के साथ 'विलय के दस्तावेज़' पर हस्ताक्षर किए, इस आश्वासन पर कि मणिपुर की आंतरिक स्वायत्तता बरकरार रखी जाएगी। महाराजा ने जून 1948 में मणिपुर में चुनाव कराए और राज्य एक संवैधानिक राजतंत्र बन गया। इस प्रकार मणिपुर भारत का पहला हिस्सा था जिसने सार्वभौमिक वयस्क मताधिकार के आधार पर चुनाव आयोजित किए।

questions.question: Baily's beads can be observed during a/an?
questions.explanation: Baily's beads can be observed during a total solar eclipse The Baily's beads effect, or diamond ring effect, is a feature of total and annular solar eclipses.

questions.question: Which fruit did Assam's Chief Minister recently declare as the state's official fruit?
questions.explanation: In February 2024, Assam's Chief Minister, Himanta Biswa Sarma, officially declared Kaji Nemu, a unique variety of lemon indigenous to Assam, as the state's official fruit. Known for its distinct aroma and antioxidant properties, Kaji Nemu has been granted a Geographical Indication (GI) tag, highlighting its unique origin and quality. > फरवरी 2024 में, असम के मुख्यमंत्री, हिमंत बिस्वा सरमा ने आधिकारिक तौर पर काजी नेमू को राज्य के आधिकारिक फल के रूप में असम के लिए स्वदेशी नींबू की एक अनूठी किस्म घोषित किया। अपनी विशिष्ट सुगंध और एंटीऑक्सीडेंट गुणों के लिए जाना जाता है, काजी नेमू को भौगोलिक संकेत जीआई टैग दिया गया है, जो इसकी अनूठी उत्पत्ति और गुणवत्ता को उजागर करता है।

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India states that 'The President, or the Governor or Rajpramukh of a State, shall not be answerable to any court for the exercise and performance of the powers and duties of his office'?
questions.explanation: Article 361 of the Constitution of India states that 'The President, or the Governor or Rajpramukh of a State, shall not be answerable to any court for the exercise and performance of the powers and duties of his office'.

questions.question: Kalbelia folk songs and dances belong to which Indian State?
questions.explanation: Kalbelia folk songs and dances belong to Rajasthan. It is performed by the Kalbelia tribe. The traditional occupation of the tribe is catching snakes and trading snake venom.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a natural acid- base indicator?
questions.explanation: Thyme leaves is not a natural acid-base indicator.

questions.question: If two numbers are 20% and 25% more than a third number, respectively, then the first number is how much per cent of the second number?
questions.explanation: Let the third number be 100. First number = 100 + {20% of 100} = 100 + 20 = 120 Second number = 100 + {25% of 100} = 100 + 25 = 125 = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970410664c66.94424360.gif) ×100 = 96%.

questions.question: The marked price of an article is ₹1,200 and it is sold at a discount of 8%. If there is a gain of 38%, then by what per cent above the cost price was the article marked?
questions.explanation: Since an 8% discount is given on the Marked Price: SP = MP – [8% of MP] SP = 1200 – [ ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970410947327.12602144.gif) × 1200] SP = 1200 - 96 SP = ₹1104 SP = CP + [38% of CP] 1104 = CP × [ ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970410ae8138.39655622.gif) ] CP = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970410cd04c1.43842661.gif) CP = ₹800 % increase = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970410e32fd2.24260876.gif) × 100 = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997041119b279.69865579.gif) × 100 = 50% Alternate Method: Equate selling price CP×138 = MP×92 CP:MP= 2:3 ---- ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704112dd763.61499886.gif) × 100 = 50%

questions.question: In ∆ABC, DE||BC and 6AE = 10EC. If AB = 12.8 units, then the value of DB [in units] is:
questions.explanation: DE ∥ BC 6AE = 10EC AE is 10x and EC is 6x AB = 12.8 units Let AD = 5x and DB = 3x. Since AB = AD + DB, we write: 5x + 3x = 12.8 8x = 12.8 x = 1.6 DB = 3x = 3{1.6} = 4.8 Thus, DB = 4.8 units.

questions.question: In what proportion should tea that costs ₹7.50 per kg be mixed with tea that costs ₹10.50 per kg so that the cost of the resultant mixture is ₹8.50 per kg?
questions.explanation: Using the method of allegation on price 7.5 10.5 8.5 2 1 = 2:1

questions.question: The average of runs scored by a player in 25 innings is 20 runs. How many runs should he score in the 26th innings so that his average is increased by 3?
questions.explanation: The average score of the player in 25 innings is 20. Total runs in 25 innings = 25 × 20 =500 To increase the average by 3, the new average after 26 innings should be 23. Total runs required in 26 innings = 26×23 =598 Runs to be scored in the 26th inning = 598-500= 98

questions.question: The difference between the selling prices with 29% profit and 21% loss is Rs.45450. What is the cost price of the item?
questions.explanation: Let cost price of an article is 100x If it is sold with 29% profit that selling price will be 129x If it is sold at 21% loss then the selling price would be 79x 129x-79x = 50x 50x= 45450 X= 909 100x= ₹90900

questions.question: Two taps A and B fill a tank in 10 minutes and 60 minutes respectively. If both the taps are opened simultaneously, after how many minutes tap A should be closed so that the tank fills up in 12 minutes ?
questions.explanation: Tap A fills the tank in 10 minutes, so the rate is ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704125d4351.34542336.gif) per minute. Tap B fills the tank in 60 minutes, so the rate is ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699704127ead80.47238243.gif) per minute We need the tank to fill in 12 minutes. A - 10 min 6 60 B- 60 min 1 B worked for 12 minutes, so the tank filled by B = 12 units Remaining tank is filled by A in the initial time = 48/6 = 8 minutes.

questions.question: Three numbers are in the ratio 5 : 8 : 9. If their sum is 1056 , what will be sum of the first 2 numbers?
questions.explanation: Let the numbers be 5x, 8x, and 9x. The sum of the numbers is: 5x + 8x + 9x = 1056 Simplify the equation: 22x = 1056 x = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970412c8a0a2.94796324.gif) = 48 48×13=624

questions.question: In 1 km linear race, P beats Q by 120 metres or 30 sec. What is the time taken by P to cover the race?
questions.explanation: Speed ratio of P : Q = 1000 : 880 = 25:22 So, Time ratio = 22:25 3 units = 30 sec 1 unit = 10 sec Time taken by P = 22 units = 220 sec

questions.question: What is the smallest five-digit prime number?
questions.explanation: 10007 is the smallest 5-digit prime number

questions.question: If a train covers a certain distance in 1 hour 24 minutes by covering two-third of the distance at 52km/hr and the rest at 65 km/hr, then find the total distance?
questions.explanation: 2D/52 + D/65 = 1 hour 24 min D/26 + D/65 = 1.4 LCM of 26 and 65 = 130 7D/130 = 1.4 D = 26km. Distance = 3D = 78 km

questions.question: A tangent is drawn from a point that is at a distance of 65 cm from centre of the circle O, and diameter is 126 cm. The length [in cm] of the tangent is:
questions.explanation: Distance of the external point from the center: d = 65 cm • Diameter of the circle: 126 cm • Radius r = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970417aa8f85.77293392.gif) = 63 cm Length of Tangent = √ 65²- 63² = √4225-3969 = √256 = 16 cm Thus, the length of the tangent is 16 cm.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error is in segment " Everyone except he". CONCEPT: • The word "except" is a preposition, and after a preposition, the objective case of a pronoun should be used. • "he" [subjective case] should be replaced with "him" [objective case] to maintain correct grammatical structure. Correct Sentence: "Everyone except him and his brother was invited to the annual function."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Soldiers are trained to _________ without question.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "Obey." Explanation: The verb "obey" means to follow commands or instructions without question, which fits the context of soldiers being trained to follow orders strictly. The phrase "obey without question" is commonly used in military and disciplinary contexts. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Observe -अवलोकन करना – To watch carefully or follow rules. Example: "Scientists observe changes in the environment." [Incorrect, because soldiers are not just watching but following orders.] • Obey -आज्ञा का पालन करना – To follow commands or rules. Example: "Students must obey the school rules." [Correct, because soldiers are trained to obey orders strictly.] • Behave -आचरण करना – To conduct oneself in a particular way. Example: "Children should behave well in class." [Incorrect, because soldiers are not just behaving but strictly following commands.]

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The letter has being written by Sheetal right now.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Is being written • The phrase "has being written" is incorrect because "being" cannot follow "has" in perfect tenses. • The correct passive voice structure for an action happening right now [present continuous] is "is being written." Note: • "Is being" is used for actions happening at the moment in the passive voice. Corrected Sentence: "The letter is being written by Sheetal right now."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: All is well that end well in the end, despite the challenges faced.
All are well that ends well when everything falls into place eventually.
All is well that ends well as long as the outcome is favourable.
All is well which ends well no matter how difficult the journey was.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is "All is well that ends well as long as the outcome is favourable." CONCEPT: ‘All’ can be used in both Countable and uncountable sense. When used as uncountable, it takes Singular Verb and when used as countable, it takes plural verb. • "All" is singular in this phrase, meaning "everything" as a whole. • The phrase "that ends well" follows proper subject-verb agreement, where "that" refers to "all" [everything] and requires a singular verb ["ends"]. • "That" is the correct relative pronoun because it introduces a defining clause referring to "all." Why the other options are incorrect? • Option[A] "end" [plural verb] is incorrect because "that" refers to "all" [singular] and needs a singular verb ["ends"]. • Option[B] "are" [plural verb] is incorrect because "all" is singular in this context. • Option[D] "which" is incorrect because "that" is preferred for defining clauses.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is "Medieval." • Medieval – मध्ययुगीन – Relating to the Middle Ages. Example: "The castle is a beautiful example of medieval architecture."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The fire broke up in the factory last night.
questions.explanation: Use "Broke out." • The phrase "broke up" means to end a relationship, a meeting, or to separate into parts, which does not fit the context of a fire starting suddenly. • The sentence suggests that a fire suddenly started in the factory, which aligns with the meaning of "broke out." Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Broke out – अचानक शुरू होना – To start suddenly, especially for fire, war, or disease. Example: "A fire broke out in the forest due to dry weather." • Broke off – अलग होना / अचानक समाप्त होना – To end something suddenly, such as an agreement or relationship. Example: "She broke off her engagement just before the wedding." • Broke in – ज़बरदस्ती घुसना – To enter a place forcefully or interrupt someone. Example: "Thieves broke in and stole valuable items from the shop." • Broke up – समाप्त करना /अलग होना – To end a relationship or separate into pieces. Example: "The school broke up for summer vacation." Hence, “Broke out" is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - The old man lived a SOLITARY life in the mountains.
questions.explanation: Sol: The word "solitary" refers to someone or something that is alone, isolated, or separate from others. In the context of the sentence, the old man is described as living a solitary life, meaning he is living alone without much social interaction. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Solitary – अकेला / एकांत में रहने वाला – Living alone or in isolation. Example: "After retiring, he chose a solitary life in the countryside." • Mystical – रहस्यमय / आध्यात्मिक – Something related to mystery, spirituality, or the supernatural. Example: "The ancient ruins had a mystical aura." • Luxurious – शानदार / विलासितापूर्ण – Having great comfort and wealth. Example: "They stayed in a luxurious hotel during their vacation." • Comfortable – आरामदायक / सुखद – Feeling physically or emotionally at ease. Example: "The new chair is very comfortable to sit on." • Lonely – अकेला / तनहा – Feeling alone, often with sadness. Example: "After moving to a new city, she felt lonely without her family." Hence, Lonely is the most appropriate synonym for Solitary.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. ELEGANCE
questions.explanation: Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Elegance – शिष्टता / सुंदरता – The quality of being stylish, graceful, and refined. Example: "Her dress exuded elegance and charm at the event." • Vulgarity – अशिष्टता / अभद्रता – The lack of good taste, refinement, or sophistication. Example: "His rude jokes showed a sense of vulgarity rather than class." • Original – मौलिक / असली – Something that is new, fresh, or authentic. Example: "His idea was truly original and creative." • Verity – सत्य / सच्चाई – The quality of being true or real. Example: "The journalist questioned the verity of the report." • Gracefulness – कोमलता / आकर्षण – The quality of moving in a smooth, attractive, and elegant way. Example: "She danced with remarkable gracefulness on the stage." Hence, "Vulgarity" is the most appropriate antonym for "Elegance."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. His coach and teammates congratulated him _______ his outstanding performance in the final match.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition to use with "congratulated" when referring to an achievement is "on." Meanings and Examples of All Options: • On – पर / के लिए – Used after "congratulate" to indicate the reason for celebration. Example: "She congratulated him on his promotion." • With – साथ में – Used to indicate an instrument or means but not for congratulations. Example: "He wrote the letter with a pen." • About – के बारे में – Used for discussing topics, but not for congratulations. Example: "We talked about the final match." • At – पर – Used for locations or specific points in time, not for congratulations. Example: "He arrived at the stadium early." Corrected Sentence: "His coach and teammates congratulated him ON his outstanding performance in the final match."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Be in the red
options: To be in a loss or debt.
Be in the list of people who are never invited.
Be in the bad books of a person.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: - To be in a loss or debt. "Be in the red" – "घाटे में होना / कर्ज़ में होना" This idiom means to have financial losses or to be in debt. It originates from accounting, where losses are traditionally recorded in red ink, while profits are written in black ink. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase comes from financial record-keeping, where negative balances or debts were marked in red ink, indicating a financial deficit. Example: "After the unexpected expenses, the company was in the red for several months." Hindi: "अनपेक्षित खर्चों के कारण कंपनी कई महीनों तक घाटे में थी।" "To be in a loss or debt" is the most appropriate meaning of "Be in the red."

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: will advance to the final round.
questions.explanation: The error is in segment " only the three first." • In English, when using ordinal numbers [first, second, third, etc.], the correct structure is: Ordinal number [first, second, etc.] → Quantity [three, four, etc.] • Incorrect: "the three first" • Correct: "the first three" • This rule applies because ordinal numbers specify position or rank, and they must come before the quantity to make sense. Correct Sentence: "Only the first three, out of the ten contestants, will advance to the final round."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. ABERRATION
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Deviation. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Aberration – विचलन / असामान्यता – A departure from what is normal, expected, or usual. Example: "His sudden outburst of anger was an aberration from his usual calm demeanour." • Thoughtless – अविचारी / असंवेदनशील – Lacking consideration for others or acting without thinking. Example: "It was thoughtless of him to ignore her feelings." • Cheerless – उदास / निराशाजनक – Lacking happiness or brightness. Example: "The room looked cheerless without any decorations." • Deviation – विचलन / अलग हटाव – A departure from the normal course or expected standard. Example: "His decision to drop out of college was a deviation from his family’s expectations." • Regular – सामान्य / नियमित – Something that follows a consistent pattern or routine. Example: "She has a regular workout schedule." Thus, “Deviation" is the most appropriate synonym for "Aberration."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- She has a PENCHANT for collecting rare books.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is- Dislike Explanation: The word "penchant" means a strong liking or preference for something. The antonym should express a lack of interest or an aversion to something. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Penchant – झुकाव / रुचि – A strong liking or natural tendency for something. Example: "She has a penchant for reading historical novels." • Fondness – लगाव / प्रेम – A deep affection or strong liking for something. Example: "He has a fondness for Italian cuisine." • Dislike – नापसंद / अरुचि – A feeling of aversion or not liking something. Example: "She has a strong dislike for loud music." • Indifference – उदासीनता / लापरवाही – A lack of interest or concern. Example: "She showed indifference toward politics." • Belief – विश्वास – A firm opinion or conviction about something. Example: "She holds a strong belief in hard work." Thus, “Dislike" is the most appropriate antonym for "Penchant."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. His conduct offended against the rules of _________ behaviour.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "His conduct offended against the rules of decent behaviour." This means that his conduct did not align with socially acceptable or proper behaviour. Explanation of the Other Options: • Dissent – "असहमति / विरोध" – A strong disagreement or opposition. Example: "The students expressed their dissent against the new school policy." • Distant – "दूर / अलग-थलग" – Far away in space or emotionally disconnected. Example: "He seemed distant during the conversation." • Descent – "वंश / अवरोहण" – The act of going downward or ancestry. Example: "She is of royal descent." • Decent – "सभ्य / शालीन" – Socially acceptable, proper, or respectable. Example: "He always speaks in a decent manner with others." Thus, "Decent" is the most appropriate answer in the given context.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A cinema show held in the afternoon
questions.explanation: Here are the explanations for all options: • Entertainment – "मनोरंजन" – Any activity that provides amusement or enjoyment. Example: "Watching movies is a great form of entertainment." • Play – "नाटक" – A theatrical performance on stage. Example: "The school organized a play based on Shakespeare’s works." • Premiere – "प्रथम प्रदर्शन" – The first official screening of a movie or event. Example: "The actors attended the premiere of their latest film." • Matinee – "दोपहर का सिनेमा शो" – A cinema or theatrical show held in the afternoon. Example: "We decided to watch a matinee show because the tickets were cheaper." Thus, "Matinee" is the most appropriate choice as it refers to a cinema show held in the afternoon.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. He had no choice but to show the white flag in the final round of the competition.
questions.explanation: Show the White Flag – आत्मसमर्पण करना या हार मान लेना "Show the white flag" means to surrender, give up, or admit defeat in a situation where one can no longer continue fighting or resisting. It is often used in competitive, conflict, or battle-related contexts. Origin of this Idiom: This phrase comes from historical battlefields where soldiers would wave a white flag to signal surrender. The white flag was a universally recognized sign that the opposing side was giving up and wanted to negotiate or stop fighting. Over time, the phrase became a metaphor for surrendering in various aspects of life, such as competitions, arguments, or challenges. Example: "After struggling with the difficult exam, I finally decided to show the white flag and leave some questions unanswered." [This means that the person gave up trying to answer all the questions because they found the exam too difficult.] Hindi Translation: "मुश्किल परीक्षा से जूझने के बाद, मैंने आखिरकार सफेद झंडा दिखाने और कुछ प्रश्नों को छोड़ने का फैसला किया।" Hence, To surrender or give up is right meaning of idiom show the white flag.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. An old unmarried woman
questions.explanation: Meanings and Examples of the Given Options: • Masochist – "मसोचिस्ट" – A person who enjoys pain or suffering, especially in a psychological or physical way. Example: "Some people enjoy extreme workouts, but that doesn't make them masochists." • Septuagenarian – "सत्तर वर्ष का व्यक्ति" – A person who is between 70 and 79 years old. Example: "Despite being a septuagenarian, my grandfather still runs every morning." • Sniper – "सटीक निशानेबाज" – A person who shoots targets from a long distance with great accuracy, usually in a military or law enforcement context. Example: "The sniper was positioned on the rooftop to ensure security during the event." • Spinster – "अविवाहित वृद्ध महिला" – An old unmarried woman, often used in a traditional or outdated sense. Example: "In old novels, a spinster was often portrayed as a lonely woman, but today, many women choose to remain unmarried." Hence, Spinster is An old unmarried woman.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. This process helps plants convert sunlight into energy. B. Without photosynthesis, life on Earth as we know it would not exist. C. During this process, carbon dioxide and water are transformed into glucose and oxygen. D. Photosynthesis is a vital biological process for plants.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: DCAB This order makes sense because: • Sentence D introduces the topic: photosynthesis as a vital process. • Sentence C explains the process: transformation of carbon dioxide and water. • Sentence A describes what photosynthesis does: converts sunlight into energy. • Sentence B concludes by emphasising the importance of photosynthesis for life on Earth. Final paragraph: "Photosynthesis is a vital biological process for plants. During this process, carbon dioxide and water are transformed into glucose and oxygen. This process helps plants convert sunlight into energy. Without photosynthesis, life on Earth as we know it would not exist."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The mechanic repaired the car yesterday.
options: The car is repaired by the mechanic yesterday.
The car will be repaired by the mechanic yesterday.
The car was repaired by the mechanic yesterday.
The car is being repaired by the mechanic yesterday.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: The car was repaired by the mechanic yesterday. Active voice structure: The mechanic [Subject] + repaired [Verb] + the car [Object] + yesterday. Passive voice structure: The car [Object] + was repaired [Verb3] + by the mechanic [by + Subject] + yesterday. This option correctly changes the voice of the sentence from active to passive, emphasising the object [the car] rather than the subject [the mechanic]. The verb tense remains consistent in the past tense with "was repaired."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He said to me, “Are you coming to the party?”
options: He asked me if I was coming to the party.
He told me that I was coming to the party.
He said to me that I am coming to the party.
He asked me if I will come to the party.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: He asked me if I was coming to the party. In indirect speech: • Interrogative sentences use verbs like "asked" or "inquired." • The reporting verb "said to" changes to "asked." • The question form "Are you coming" changes to a statement "if I was coming." The tense changes from present continuous ["are coming"] to past continuous ["was coming"].

questions.question: Medicines have become an element of surprise and a cause of concern. This is because they have begun to _____1____ diseases instead of curing them. No one has a solution. The doctors are perplexed and the patients are ____2____. The outcome of intensive research and in-depth studies has ____3____ everyone. These findings show that unnecessary use of medicines has created a trend in which people not only take medicines for every small ailment, but also advise others to do so. People think that they should take strong drugs, irrespective of ____4____ the disease is minor or major. Nowadays, a shelf of medicines is found in every house. This practice is not merely confined ____5____ general medicines, but has now spread even to antibiotics. The literal meaning of the word antibiotics is – ‘against life’. In fact, due to their misuse, they have really begun acting against our life. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Aggravate," means to make a problem or situation worse. In the context of the given sentence, "aggravate" fits perfectly. It suggests that medicines are making diseases worse instead of curing them, which aligns with the sentence's intended meaning. Let’s see the other options:- "Ameliorate," means to improve or make something better. In the context of the given sentence, "ameliorate" would imply that medicines are actually improving or making diseases better, which is the opposite of what the sentence intends. Therefore, this option doesn't fit the context. "Alleviate," means to reduce the severity of a problem or pain. In the given sentence, "alleviate" would suggest that medicines are reducing the severity of diseases, which is not what the sentence intends. The sentence implies that medicines are having a negative impact, rather than a positive one. "Annihilate," means to completely destroy or wipe out something. In the given sentence, "annihilate" is too extreme. While it's true that medicines can have negative effects, the sentence doesn't suggest that they're completely destroying or wiping out diseases. This option is too drastic and doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: Medicines have become an element of surprise and a cause of concern. This is because they have begun to _____1____ diseases instead of curing them. No one has a solution. The doctors are perplexed and the patients are ____2____. The outcome of intensive research and in-depth studies has ____3____ everyone. These findings show that unnecessary use of medicines has created a trend in which people not only take medicines for every small ailment, but also advise others to do so. People think that they should take strong drugs, irrespective of ____4____ the disease is minor or major. Nowadays, a shelf of medicines is found in every house. This practice is not merely confined ____5____ general medicines, but has now spread even to antibiotics. The literal meaning of the word antibiotics is – ‘against life’. In fact, due to their misuse, they have really begun acting against our life. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Annoyed," means feeling irritated, frustrated, or vexed. In the context of the given sentence, "annoyed" fits perfectly. The patients are likely feeling annoyed or frustrated because the medicines are not curing their diseases, but rather making them worse. This emotion is a natural response to a situation where someone is expecting relief, but instead, faces more problems. Therefore, "annoyed" is a suitable choice to describe the patients' emotional state. Let’s see other options- "Blessed," means feeling grateful, fortunate, or happy. In the given sentence, "blessed" doesn't fit the context. The patients are unlikely to be feeling grateful or fortunate when the medicines are making their diseases worse. This option is too positive for the situation described, and it doesn't accurately convey the patients' emotional state. "Suspected," means believed to be guilty or responsible for something. In the given sentence, "suspected" doesn't fit the context. The patients are not being accused of anything, and the sentence is describing their emotional state, not their guilt or responsibility. This option is too unrelated to the situation described, and it doesn't make sense in the context of the sentence. "Elated," means feeling extremely happy or proud. In the given sentence, "elated" doesn't fit the context. The patients are unlikely to be feeling extremely happy or proud when the medicines are making their diseases worse. This option is too positive for the situation described, and it doesn't accurately convey the patients' emotional state.

questions.question: Medicines have become an element of surprise and a cause of concern. This is because they have begun to _____1____ diseases instead of curing them. No one has a solution. The doctors are perplexed and the patients are ____2____. The outcome of intensive research and in-depth studies has ____3____ everyone. These findings show that unnecessary use of medicines has created a trend in which people not only take medicines for every small ailment, but also advise others to do so. People think that they should take strong drugs, irrespective of ____4____ the disease is minor or major. Nowadays, a shelf of medicines is found in every house. This practice is not merely confined ____5____ general medicines, but has now spread even to antibiotics. The literal meaning of the word antibiotics is – ‘against life’. In fact, due to their misuse, they have really begun acting against our life. Which of the following options can NOT be filled in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Mocked" means being ridiculed, teased, or laughed at. When someone is mocked, they are being treated with contempt or disdain. In the context of the sentence, "mocked" doesn't fit at all. The outcome of intensive research and in-depth studies is unlikely to be mocking people. The sentence is describing a serious situation where people are trying to understand the results of the research, and "mocked" is too informal and unrelated to the context. Hence, Mocked cannot be filled the blank. Let’s see other options - Baffled, Puzzled and Startled all are synonyms which mean being completely surprised, puzzled or perplexed. In the context of the sentence, all of them could fit perfectly. The outcome of intensive research and in-depth studies could have left everyone surprised, unable to understand or explain the results.

questions.question: Medicines have become an element of surprise and a cause of concern. This is because they have begun to _____1____ diseases instead of curing them. No one has a solution. The doctors are perplexed and the patients are ____2____. The outcome of intensive research and in-depth studies has ____3____ everyone. These findings show that unnecessary use of medicines has created a trend in which people not only take medicines for every small ailment, but also advise others to do so. People think that they should take strong drugs, irrespective of ____4____ the disease is minor or major. Nowadays, a shelf of medicines is found in every house. This practice is not merely confined ____5____ general medicines, but has now spread even to antibiotics. The literal meaning of the word antibiotics is – ‘against life’. In fact, due to their misuse, they have really begun acting against our life. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - "Whether" is a conjunction used to introduce a choice between two alternatives. In this sentence, it's used to indicate that people think they should take strong drugs regardless of the severity of the disease. Here's a brief explanation of the other options: Whenever: refers to the time or occasion when something happens. It doesn't fit the context of the sentence. Wherever: refers to the place or location where something happens. It doesn't fit the context of the sentence. Where: refers to the place or location where something happens. It doesn't fit the context of the sentence.

questions.question: Medicines have become an element of surprise and a cause of concern. This is because they have begun to _____1____ diseases instead of curing them. No one has a solution. The doctors are perplexed and the patients are ____2____. The outcome of intensive research and in-depth studies has ____3____ everyone. These findings show that unnecessary use of medicines has created a trend in which people not only take medicines for every small ailment, but also advise others to do so. People think that they should take strong drugs, irrespective of ____4____ the disease is minor or major. Nowadays, a shelf of medicines is found in every house. This practice is not merely confined ____5____ general medicines, but has now spread even to antibiotics. The literal meaning of the word antibiotics is – ‘against life’. In fact, due to their misuse, they have really begun acting against our life. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The phrase "confined to" is a common idiomatic expression in English. "Confined to" means limited or restricted to something. It indicates that something is restricted or narrowed down to a specific area, group, or scope. For example: "The disease is confined to the remote village." i.e. the disease is limited to that village and hasn't spread elsewhere. "The fire was confined to the kitchen." i.e. the fire was limited to the kitchen and didn't spread to other parts of the building. In the context of the our sentence: The phrase "confined to" means that the practice was initially limited to general medicines, but it has now expanded or spread to include antibiotics as well.
